name: Legendre's Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The closed algebraic structure where every positive integer n has a unique prime factorization, and the p-adic valuation v_p(n!) is fully determined by Legendre's formula.

VINIT.justification: Before any analysis, n exists as a bare quantity without decomposition or valuation. Void state: the integer before its prime structure is revealed.

TANCH.justification: Terminal anchor: the hermetic boundary that guarantees every positive integer has a unique prime factorization. This is the constitutional court that closes and validates the entire system.

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism: the directed transformation that ascends through successive powers of p, accumulating the exponent v_p(n!) step by step. Growth cycle toward the complete valuation.

AREV.justification: Contravariant reverse: the backward morphism that descends through the factorial hierarchy. Coagula: the unwinding of multiplicative structure back toward unity.

CLINK.chosen_element: Composition of valuations: v_p(n!) = Σ ⌊n/p^k⌋ for all primes p
CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining: each term in the sum is composed with the next, building the complete exponent. Root→derived form: the base formula generates all specific instances.

IMSCRIB.justification: Self-reference: the element recognizes itself. The valuation is its own witness. The stone that knows itself: Legendre's formula is self-validating through the Fundamental Theorem.

FSPLIT.chosen_element: Prime p divides n! or p does not divide n!
FSPLIT.justification: Co-multiplication: the factorial n! branches into two exhaustive cases: either p appears in the prime factorization (v_p(n!) > 0) or it does not (v_p(n!) = 0). Separatio: the binary fork of divisibility.

FFUSE.chosen_element: Reconstitution of n! from its complete prime factorization
FFUSE.justification: Multiplication: all branches (one for each prime p, each with its exponent v_p(n!)) reconstitute the original n! exactly. Conjunctio: FFUSE(FSPLIT(n!)) = n!. The Fundamental Theorem guarantees this fusion.

EVALT.justification: True/affirmative branch: the positive evaluation. When p ≤ n, the prime p definitely appears in n!, and v_p(n!) ≥ 1. Success state: the condition is satisfied.

EVALF.chosen_element: p > n (prime p does not divide n!)
EVALF.justification: False/negative branch: the negative evaluation. When p > n, no factor of n! contains p, so v_p(n!) = 0. Failure state: the prime is absent.

ENGAGR.chosen_element: The boundary case p = n when n is prime
ENGAGR.justification: Paradice: both simultaneously held without resolution. When n is prime, n appears exactly once in n! (as the factor n itself), yet the formula ⌊n/n⌋ + ⌊n/n²⌋ + ... = 1 + 0 + 0 + ... captures this in a way that holds both the presence and the singularity in tension. Bicameral compromise: the prime is both a factor and a boundary.

IFIX.chosen_element: Each computed exponent v_p(n!) is recorded in the prime factorization of n!
IFIX.justification: ROM fixation: permanent, append-only, cannot be undone. Once v_p(n!) is calculated via Legendre's formula, it is fixed as part of the canonical factorization. Egyptian determinatives: the exponent is a permanent marker of n!'s structure.

phase_2.split_input: n! (the factorial as a unified integer)
split_outputs: T-arm: {p : p ≤ n, p prime} with exponents v_p(n!) > 0
F-arm: {p : p > n, p prime} with exponents v_p(n!) = 0

phase_3.void_description: 00 — n before any prime analysis; the integer as bare quantity, undecomposed, before Legendre's formula is applied.
phase_3.true_description: 01 — p ≤ n; the prime p divides n!, v_p(n!) > 0, the affirmative branch is active, the exponent is positive and recorded.
phase_3.false_description: 10 — p > n; the prime p does not divide n!, v_p(n!) = 0, the negative branch is active, the exponent is zero and fixed.
phase_3.both_description: 11 — the boundary paradice when n is prime: n appears as both a factor in n! and as the upper limit of the summation, held in simultaneous tension without resolution.

steps.domain_action: n is given as a bare positive integer, unanalyzed, void state (00).
The Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic closes the system: every n has a unique prime factorization.
n! branches into two exhaustive cases: primes p ≤ n (T-arm, divisibility true) and primes p > n (F-arm, divisibility false).
For each prime p ≤ n, the affirmative evaluation: p divides n!, so v_p(n!) ≥ 1. Register state T(01).
Forward morphism on T-arm: compute v_p(n!) = ⌊n/p⌋ + ⌊n/p²⌋ + ⌊n/p³⌋ + ... by ascending through powers of p.
Compose the sum: each term ⌊n/p^k⌋ chains to the next, building the complete exponent.
Record v_p(n!) as a permanent exponent in the prime factorization of n!. Append-only, cannot be undone.
For each prime p > n, the negative evaluation: p does not divide n!, so v_p(n!) = 0. Register state F(10).
Reverse morphism on F-arm: descend through the factorial hierarchy; no factors of n! contain p, so the exponent remains zero.
Record v_p(n!) = 0 as a permanent absence in the factorization. Determinative marker: p is not present.
At the boundary case where n is prime: n appears as a factor in n! (true) and as the summation limit (false), held simultaneously in paradice B(11).
Identity self-reference: v_p(n!) = v_p(n!). The valuation recognizes itself through Legendre's formula.
Reconstitute n! from all its prime exponents: n! = ∏_p p^{v_p(n!)}. The Frobenius constraint is satisfied: FFUSE(FSPLIT(n!)) = n!.
Compose the final factorization: each prime power p^{v_p(n!)} chains with the next, recovering the original n!.
Self-reference loop closes: the reconstructed n! is identical to the input n!. The system recognizes its own closure.
Terminal anchor: the Fundamental Theorem validates that this factorization is unique and complete. The system is hermetically sealed.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Legendre's formula: the algorithm that translates the domain intention (find the exponent of prime p in n!) into the operation sequence ⌊n/p⌋ + ⌊n/p²⌋ + ⌊n/p³⌋ + ...
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Prime divisibility: the communication protocol between the factorial n! and each prime p. The query 'does p divide n!?' is answered by the exponent v_p(n!).
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The prime factorization table: each (p, v_p(n!)) pair is stored as a permanent record. The exponents are the state of the system.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The enumeration of primes: for each prime p in order (2, 3, 5, 7, ...), compute v_p(n!) via the ascending summation, then record it. Sequential processing of all primes up to n.
phase_5.alfs_store: The Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic: the canonical reference that guarantees uniqueness and completeness of every prime factorization. The bootstrap store that validates all exponents.

phase_6.cycle_cost: Per prime p: O(log_p n) arithmetic operations (one division per power of p). Total cost: O(π(n) log n) where π(n) is the prime-counting function.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: n! as an unanalyzed integer; no exponents computed; register state void (00).
phase_6.post_cycle_state: n! fully decomposed into prime factors with all exponents v_p(n!) recorded; register state true (01) for p ≤ n, false (10) for p > n, both (11) at boundaries.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The cycle is entropy-conservative because Legendre's formula is a deterministic, reversible mapping from n to the complete set of exponents {v_p(n!)}. The Frobenius constraint FFUSE(FSPLIT(n!)) = n! ensures that no information is lost: the factorization can be perfectly reconstructed. The system is a closed algebraic loop with no dissipation.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → CLINK → IFIX → EVALF → AREV → IFIX → ENGAGR → IMSCRIB → FFUSE → CLINK → IMSCRIB → TANCH
-- Class: Legendre's Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(9,2,3,2)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=16
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(2, 12)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑚  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [3] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [4] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [5] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [6] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑚 → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [7] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [8] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [9] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑚 → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [10] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [11] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑚 → 𐑙  | identity — self-imscription
--   [12] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑱  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [13] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑙 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [14] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑡  | identity — self-imscription
--   [15] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑠 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def legendres_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (n is given as a bare positive integer, unanalyzed, void state (00).)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑚)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (The Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic closes the system: every n has a unique...)
  -- FSPLIT [2] (gran := 𐑚) (n! branches into two exhaustive cases: primes p ≤ n (T-arm, divisibility true) and primes p > n (F-arm, divisibility false).) / FFUSE [12] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (4 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [3] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (For each prime p ≤ n, the affirmative evaluation: p divides n!, so v_p(n!) ≥ ...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Forward morphism on T-arm: compute v_p(n!) = ⌊n/p⌋ + ⌊n/p²⌋ + ⌊n/p³⌋ + ... by...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Compose the sum: each term ⌊n/p^k⌋ chains to the next, building the complete ...)
        (.arrow 𐑭 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Record v_p(n!) as a permanent exponent in the prime factorization of n!. Appe...)
      -- F-branch (5 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (For each prime p > n, the negative evaluation: p does not divide n!, so v_p(n...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Reverse morphism on F-arm: descend through the factorial hierarchy; no factor...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑭 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Record v_p(n!) = 0 as a permanent absence in the factorization. Determinative...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [10] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (At the boundary case where n is prime: n appears as a factor in n! (true) and...)
        (.arrow 𐑠 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [11] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Identity self-reference: v_p(n!) = v_p(n!). The valuation recognizes itself t...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [12]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [12] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Reconstitute n! from all its prime exponents: n! = ∏_p p^{v_p(n!)}. The Frobenius constraint is satisfied: FFUSE(FSPLIT(n!)) = n!.)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [13] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Compose the final factorization: each prime power p^{v_p(n!)} chains with the...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑡)  -- [14] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Self-reference loop closes: the reconstructed n! is identical to the input n!...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑼)  -- [15] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Terminal anchor: the Fundamental Theorem validates that this factorization is...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def legendres_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (legendres_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def legendres_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (legendres_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem legendres_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
